caption:
Here’s part 2 of the best eats that I had on my trip to JAPAN!

5. Cremia soft serve (multiple locations)
4. Tuna stand at Tsukiji Market (Tokyo)
3. Imo Pippi (Tokyo)
2. Tonkatsu Marushichi (Tokyo)
1. Men-Ya Inoichi (Kyoto)

Honorable Mention
- Iyoshi Cola (Tokyo)
- Giraffa (Tokyo)
- Hatoya Matcha (Multiple locations)
- 7-Eleven (everywhere)
- Musk melon (got it at my hotel buffets)

And check out my Japan playlist to see my video with number 6-10 favorite eats!

#japaneats #japantravelguide
#wheretoeatjapan
transcript: Here's part 2 of the top 10 things I ate on my 3 week trip across Japan as someone who planned his entire trip around trying the best foods. I'm going to let you know where to go and what to get. So in my last video, I shared numbers 6 through 10, so now it's time for the top 5. Number 5 is soft serve from a brand called Cremia. And while they don't have actual stores, you can get it in many places across Japan. You can tell a place sells Cremia soft serve from the iconic cone that it's on, which is almost more like a sugar cookie. This is the best soft serve I've ever had. The vanilla isn't actually vanilla. It's milk flavored and it's incredible. Never had anything like it and I had it every chance I could. Number 4 is at the Tsukiji Market in Tokyo. Yes, there will be a line for the popular tuna nigiri stand, but it moves fast and it's so incredibly worth it. You won't find fresher tuna than this. Also, this tray was like $15 US. And if you missed it, I made a full video on where to eat at the Tsukiji Market if you're interested. Number 3 is Imo Peepee in Tokyo, which specializes in Japanese sweet potato. Their creme brulee sweet potato was insane. The custard perfect and sweet potato is so creamy and dense that it's an absolute must-get. And if you want an amazing sweet potato just as is, go to Tsubo Yakima in Ginza, which only sells one thing, slow roasted Japanese sweet potatoes. That's it. The number 2 bite of food is one I had to wait over 1 hour in line for because their entire restaurant only has like 8 seats. It's Tonkatsu Marusichi in Tokyo for their katsudan, which is pork katsu, scrambled eggs, and rice. This might be the best fried pork I've ever had and mixed with the soft scrambled eggs and rice. I'll dream about this dish. Doesn't matter how long you have to wait, go. And before I share the number 1 dish, I have to run through some honorable mention which just missed out on the top 10 list. Yoshi Cola in Tokyo makes a freshly made cola which was a favorite of mine. Jirafa in Tokyo makes something called a curry pon, which is a crispy deep fried milk bread filled with a rich umami Japanese curry and gooey mozzarella cheese. It was so crispy, light, flavorful, you gotta try it. Our favorite matcha from the trip was from Hatoya Matcha in Tokyo. Both Chrissy and I agreed this was our favorite. Definitely get it with the matcha cold foam. That's key. And of course, the 7-Eleven egg salad sando has to be on this list. The hype is real. And pro tip, add a Family Mart fried chicken for the ultimate cheap delicious sandwich. And I can't forget, the melon in Japan is just next level. You gotta try it. It's 100 times better than the one in the States. Just listen to the juice level. And my number 1 eat from Japan is from this spot in Kyoto. Menya Inoichi. They make ramen, but this isn't normal ramen. It's Michelin rated ramen. I got the dark soy ramen with grilled A4 Wagyu beef. In a country with so many great eats, this was my favorite bite and spoonful of the trip. The chefs here are just ramen magicians. I traveled 24 hours back to Kyoto just for this. But not everything in Japan was a hit. In my next video, I'm going to share some popular spots in Japan that I personally thought were overrated. So make sure to follow for more food adventures.
